title: Everdeep Story-Web Plan
description: A declarative generation plan whose roles resolve to entity references — the connected-generation contract (ARCHITECTURE.md §5.6, Q14). Role seeds derive as <ownerSeedPath>/role:<roleId> (CONTRACTS.md §1). Plan authoring is continuous content work, like tables.

theme.description: Theme tags flow into every role's generation (dungeon motif, #tag-filtered monsters, loot). May itself be rolled from a table.

fromTable.description: Table id that rolls the theme tag set

source.description: mint: create a ghost (Phase B). reuse: resolve to an existing world entity matching filters, with user consent affordance (Phase E). either: prefer reuse, fall back to mint.

generator.description: composite:/generator: id used when minting; defaults to the kind's primary generator

relatedTo.description: Role id — reuse candidates must relate to this role's entity

placement.description: Where the minted entity lands on the map. Used ONCE at mint; anchors are freely movable afterward (placedBy records provenance).

nearRole.description: Anchor within range of this role's anchor

site.description: Also mint a ghost site at the anchor (dungeon/interior), themed by plan tags

contains.description: Role ids physically placed inside this role's entity/site — the quest's prize inside the lair's dungeon (Q14). Containment roles become entity children and, when the container has a site, get a cell entityId.

parentRole.description: Containment tree parent among roles (e.g. keeper's parent is the tavern)

relations.description: Relations minted between roles (villain memberOf faction).

body.description: Block templates for the owning entity's page. Text may use {@role:<id>} tokens, rewritten to {@e <resolvedId>} mentions at resolution (atomic with the mint — CONTRACTS.md §6).
